learn.title: Why Estimate Products?
learn.content: Estimating before you multiply helps you check if your final answer is reasonable. It also helps when you need a quick rough answer.
learn.tts: Estimating before you multiply helps you check if your final answer is reasonable. It also helps when you need a quick rough answer.

learn.title: Round First
learn.content: Round the larger factor to a friendly number (nearest ten, hundred, or thousand). Keep the single-digit factor the same.
learn.tts: Round the larger factor to a friendly number (nearest ten, hundred, or thousand). Keep the single-digit factor the same.

learn.title: Multiply
learn.content: After rounding, multiply using basic facts and zeros. 4 × 78 → 4 × 80 = 320.
learn.tts: After rounding, multiply using basic facts and zeros. 4 × 78 → 4 × 80 = 320.

learn.title: Choose the Place
learn.content: Round to the place that makes the number simple but close: 287 → 300 (hundred), 4,213 → 4,000 (thousand).
learn.tts: Round to the place that makes the number simple but close: 287 → 300 (hundred), 4,213 → 4,000 (thousand).

learn.title: Compare Estimates
learn.content: If you round up, the estimate is higher than the real answer. If you round down, it's lower. Know which way your estimate goes.
learn.tts: If you round up, the estimate is higher than the real answer. If you round down, it's lower. Know which way your estimate goes.

learn.title: Use to Check
learn.content: Use an estimate to verify a multiplication. If your answer and estimate are far apart, something went wrong.
learn.tts: Use an estimate to verify a multiplication. If your answer and estimate are far apart, something went wrong.
